title: HGV Driver (Appliance Deliveries) | Ballycoolin, Dublin 11
description: **Join a Growing Team – More Than Just Driving** Gencat Appliances are a leader in providing third party logistical services to appliance and vending manufacturers. From our warehouse/workshop in Dublin 15 we deliver, install, commission, service and repair appliances. Our workshop is an approved service centre for laundry, floorcare and catering appliances by a number of leading manufacturers. We are currently recruiting a Rigid HGV Driver for local and national deliveries based out of our Ballycoolin depot. This is a hands\-on role ideal for someone who enjoys variety combining driving with customer interaction, appliance handling, and light installation work. If you have experience delivering or installing home appliances (e.g. washing machines, ovens, fridges) or vending machines or come from a technical/repair background, this role offers a great opportunity to build a long\-term career with progression into technical or supervisory roles. **Why Join Us?** * Training provided – appliance installation \& product knowledge * Career progression – opportunities into senior driver, technical, or operational roles * Varied, active role – not just driving * Supportive team environment * Competitive salary based on experience **Your Role** * Carry out deliveries of appliances to homes and retail locations * Safely load, unload, and handle appliances * Install and service appliances (training provided) * Perform basic checks or minor adjustments on installed units * Provide excellent customer service at point of delivery * Complete delivery paperwork and maintain accurate records * Ensure vehicle checks and compliance with road safety regulations **What We’re Looking For** * Full, clean HGV licence (Category C1 or C) * Experience in multi\-drop deliveries (preferred) * Experience with appliances, installation, or repair work (highly desirable) * Good level of English for customer interaction and documentation * Strong work ethic, reliability, and a practical, hands\-on attitude * Comfortable working on your own or as part of a two\-person delivery team **Build Your Future With Us** This role is ideal for drivers who want to expand their skillset beyond driving. We provide structured training and real opportunities to move into technical, training, or management roles as the business grows. If you’re a reliable, customer\-focused driver looking for a local role with variety and progression, we’d love to hear from you. Pay: €30,906\.49\-€50,993\.05 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * On\-site parking * Sick pay Language: * fluent English (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: HR Graduate Programme 2026
description: NYX Hotels is giving laidback luxury: we are THE destination! Our NYX team (we call them City Lovers!) is a diverse bunch of vibrant people, who are on a mission to make NYX the first\-choice for guests looking for a high\-end stay with a unique, urban feel. NYX Hotels is a part of the Leonardo Hotels portfolio. Have you recently graduated and are wondering what’s next? We are offering HR graduates the chance to develop an amazing career in HR in hospitality! With over 55 hotels in the UK \& Ireland we have a fantastic team of over 5,000 people who we want to feel welcome, rewarded and enjoy coming to work with us everyday. We are one of the fastest growing hotel chains with an exciting opportunity to not only take part in our Human Resources Graduate Programme but also grow within an ever\-expanding company to achieve your career goals. **The Graduate Programme** * This is rotational programme covering all areas of the HR department such as generalist HR, recruitment, Group HR, L\&D and much more! * You will gain experience and insight into employment law across the UK and Ireland * Extensive personal and professional development is provided throughout the programme with training workshops, webinars and project work. * Fixed term 18\-month contract with the aim to be in a permanent role by the end of the programme. * Opportunity to work hybridly with the opportunity to work one day from home per week. * We will consider base location in any of the 56 locations across the UK and Ireland that we have hotels **What we are looking for?** * Flexibility on location to get the best out of your programme * Passion for hospitality * A proactive and positive attitude * Resilient and strong time management skills * A degree or equivalent in HR or CIPD Level 5 * Ideally you will have worked in a Hotel/Leisure/ or Hospitality setting * Right to work in the UK \& Ireland * Ability and willingness to travel across the UK and Ireland, sometimes requiring overnight stays **What's next?** * Apply online here today! * Applications close **June 2026** * We will be in touch by **June/****July 2026** * You will take part in a 2\-step process – 1 assessment centre and if successful, a final interview with a HR Manager * Opportunities to start with us from August/September with the graduate programme commencing November **2026** Some of the perks our HR Graduate Programme 2026 could enjoy include:* Special rates on Leonardo Hotel rooms across the UK \& Europe * Company\-wide recognition scheme: you could earn vouchers to spend on a wide range of high street shops * Talent referral scheme * Thank You Week: from ice cream trucks to yoga classes and lots in between! * Wellbeing Calendar * Ongoing job\-related training programmes with clear paths for progression We’re committed to creating a workplace where every individual—regardless of background, identity, or lived experience—is welcomed, valued, and respected for who they are. Belonging means embracing our differences and ensuring everyone feels seen, heard, and empowered. Why come join us as a HR Graduate Programme 2026? At NYX Hotels, we look after our City Lovers just as well as we look after our guests. Once you’re part of the NYX team, you’re part of the Leonardo Hotels community of over 55 hotels across the UK \& Ireland. Our regular Learning Bites, “Come Join Us Guest Care” training and Wellbeing webinars all help your progress with us. With support on tap, top\-notch practical training and clear progression you’ll be on track for a brilliant future in Hospitality!

title: HR Operations & Insights Analyst
description: This role is ideal for an ambitious HR Operations professional with a strong generalist background. Someone who is proactive, enjoys improving processes, spotting trends in data, and partnering with leaders to help drive informed decision\-making. A fantastic opportunity to join a high\-performing, commercially\-driven environment where standards are high and execution matters, and where your work will have a direct, visible impact across the business. This is not a transactional HR role. We are looking for someone who can look at a spreadsheet and see patterns, risk areas and opportunities. You will influence processes, provide insight and help shape the HR and reward approach across the business, making this a role that offers real stretch and visibility. **The Role** This is a broad HR Operations position with exposure across: * Payroll governance and compliance * HR reporting and workforce analytics * Recruitment process management * HRIS oversight * Global benefits administration * Annual compensation review support * Market benchmarking and reward data analysis You will work closely with HR Business Partners and business managers, providing insight, not just administration. **About You** You are: * A strong HR Operations / Generalist with 5\+ years’ experience * Excellent in Excel, comfortable with modelling, pivot tables, and trend analysis * Naturally curious and commercially aware * Confident partnering with stakeholders * Detail\-focused but able to see the bigger picture * Motivated to grow your expertise in reward and analytics * You enjoy learning, taking ownership and operating in an environment where standards are high and expectations are clear. **Why This Role?** * Exposure to global HR operations within an international organisation * Opportunity to deepen reward and analytics capability while remaining broad in scope * Work in a high\-performing, commercially\-driven environment where your work is visible and impactful * A fast\-paced, ambitious, and collaborative team offering real development and stretch opportunities If you are a driven HR Operations professional who thrives on analysis, enjoys partnering with the business and wants a role that combines operational depth with insight and influence, this is the perfect next step. If this role has piqued your interest, **apply today** or **reach out for a confidential chat** to discuss the opportunity further.

title: HSEQ Graduate
description: **Circet are now recruiting for our 12\- month Graduate Programme launching in September 2026 across our various office regions in Ireland and the UK!** We are currently looking for graduates who are eager to learn on the job, put their skills to the test and are driven to succeed. title: Human Resources Administrator
description: **Job Title** : Human Resources Administrator **Role Type:** Full\-Time, Permanent **Position Reports To:** Head Human Resources **Department:** Human Resources **Location:** AGL Head Office, Maple House, Temple Road, Blackrock, Co. Dublin. **Background:** Allianz Global Life dac (“AGL”) is a rapidly growing cross\-border life insurer with products sold into the French, Italian and German markets, and, more recently Greek and Lithuanian markets. It also reinsures a portfolio of variable annuities sold in Taiwan and Japan. Its goal is to expand into other markets over the coming years, while also continuing to expand its product range. It currently writes unit linked, variable annuity and protection business. **Main Purpose of the Job:** A vacancy has arisen for a HR Administrator within Allianz Global Life’s Human Resources function, which will be responsible for all HR administration tasks associated with the full employee lifecycle. This role will support the Head of Human Resources, HR Business Partner, reward \& Data Analyst and Learning and Development specialist, therefore gaining exposure to many key HR processes. **Key Performance Measures:** * Act as the primary point of contact for general HR inquiries and requests via the HR inbox or other channels, ensuring that accurate and timely responses are provided to stakeholders in line with local and Group policies and procedures. * Fully responsible for administrative tasks related to the employee life cycle, ensuring high\-quality documentation for processes such as contracts, probation, pay reviews, and target bonus letters. * Work with internal business units and vendors during onboarding, crossboarding, and offboarding activities to ensure company equipment is delivered or collected and systems are updated in accordance with internal SLAs. * Extract weekly, monthly, and quarterly time and attendance reports and liaise with managers to ensure timely mandatory approvals, including year\-end processes. * Assist in reviewing data for internal, external, and payroll audit requests. * Support all stakeholders in the HR department by coordinating Learning \& Development, Engagement, and wellbeing activities. * Assist the Data \& Reward Analyst with payroll and employee benefit administration, including medical insurance plans, pension schemes, share schemes, TaxSaver, and Cycle to Work programs. This job description shall not limit the duties which may be assigned to this role. The successful candidate shall be required to perform on a temporary or permanent basis such additional or different duties that the Company may reasonably assign to them from time\-to\-time. **Key Customers:** *Internal –* HR Colleagues, Compliance, Employees, People Managers, Heads of Function, CEO. *External* – Allianz Group, External Service Providers (e.g. Agencies, Pension, Medical Insurance). **Business Competencies:** *Education and Qualifications:* * 3rd level qualification in HR, or related subject. * Associate of CIPD or working towards. *Professional Competence:* * 1 year HR administration / coordination experience in the insurance / financial services industry or other highly regulated environment. *Product \& Technical Knowledge:* * Understanding / awareness of relevant employment legislation applicable to HR administration. * Understanding of best\-in\-class HR administration processes and procedures. * Proficient in HRIS (Success Factors, Strandum, or similar HRIS). * Excellent knowledge in Microsoft Office suite (Excel in particular). *Management:* * Ownership of all HR administration / coordination processes, ensuring efficient and effective best\-in\-class HR service delivery to our internal and external customers. *Commercial Awareness:* * Awareness of the additional responsibilities and high standards that a HR Function must meet within a life assurance is advantageous. **Personal Competencies:** *Personal Impact:* * Strives to provides outputs of a high standard and takes pride in meeting these standards. * Pro\-active, and demonstrated experience using initiative. * Strong organisation, prioritisation, and time\-management skills. *Intellectual Effectiveness:* * A natural problem\-solver, and critical thinker. * Ability to see beyond a transactional task, and understand the teams combined end goal. *Communication:* * Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal. Fluency in written and spoken English. *Dealing with People:* * Demonstrated ability to build successful working relationships with, and the manage expectations of, a wide variety of our internal and external customers / stakeholders.

title: IT Support Analyst
description: ITSUP014819\_1 **We’re hiring for an IT Support Analyst to support our growing Technology team!** To keep up with our growth in the UK and Ireland, we’re looking for a dedicated **IT Support Analyst** to join our growing Technology team. You will act as the first point of contact for employees seeking technical assistance for technology issues, providing technical support over the phone, in\-person, or other means of communications while maintaining high levels of customer service. **We’re Looking For Someone With** * 2\+ years of experience working for a fast\-paced corporate level Service Desk * Good knowledge of Windows Operating System (Win10 / 11\) * Good knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (2013, 2016, O365\) * Basic understanding of networking * Familiarity with mobile devices (smartphones/tablets) * Clean driving license * Excellent communication skills with ability to problem solve, quickly identify and adapt to end users * Technical abilities while maintaining the highest levels of customer service * Critical thinking in a fast\-paced environment with ability to be flexible * Highly dependable with exceptional teamwork mentality **Using These Skills, You’ll Be** * Providing technical support and hardware management across Ireland based offices * Providing support for all Technology products and services * Assisting with management of mobile devices for NFP Application usage * Executing all technical onboarding and offboarding duties * Testing, documenting, and assisting with corporate initiatives as assigned by Manager * Following standard Support Technician operating procedures; accurately logging all * Supporting Technician requests using NFP ticketing system, ServiceNow **Who is NFP?** With Over 600 People In The UK And Ireland And Over 8,000 Employees Worldwide, NFP Is Part Of The Aon Group And Specialise In Helping Businesses In Four Core Areas * Insurance (helping them manage key risks) * Health and safety (supporting them to create a safer workplace for their employees) * Employee benefits (helping them reward their people more effectively) * HR, people and talent (supporting employers and their people to thrive through changes and challenges) **What You’ll Love About Working Here** * working in a dynamic, fast\-paced organisation in an exciting industry * the opportunity to do globally impactful work from day one * learning from industry and business line specialists with decades of experience * a huge variety of projects to work on and challenges to solve * our People First culture, which illustrates our commitment to your wellbeing and development, not just as an employee but as a human being * a rich suite of employee benefits and out\-of\-work perk **The Great Benefits We Offer** **Finances** It’s important to know you’re paid fairly for the hard work you put in, which is why we complete regular reviews to keep your salary in line as you progress. Plus, we offer initiatives to support and give you financial peace of mind. * Matched employer pension contributions * Life Assurance and Group Income Protection * Lifestyle discounts for well\-known brands **Work\-life balance** We appreciate the importance of your life outside of work and the benefits of an effective work\-life balance. That’s why our policies and culture respect your ability to be flexible, helping you be your best self both in and out of the office. * Opportunity for hybrid working * Generous annual leave allowance **Health and wellbeing** We firmly believe in supporting the whole person, not just the employee. At NFP, you have access to a number of initiatives and resources designed to support your physical and mental health and wellbeing. * Comprehensive Private healthcare * Healthcare cash plan * Additional days off throughout the year to focus on your wellbeing **Charity and community work** At NFP, your sense of purpose will stretch far beyond your job role. With numerous opportunities to support charities and local initiatives close to our heart, you’ll be able to join us in making a real difference. * Numerous charity fundraising challenges and events throughout the year * Opportunities to volunteer and give back to the community * Award\-winning apprenticeship program, helping local schoolchildren take their first steps into the world of work **Social** It’s not all hard work; we also love to let our hair down. By providing plenty of opportunities to socialise with your colleagues throughout the year, we help ensure our people get on personally as well as professionally. * Two large employee events every year for summer and Christmas * Enjoy out\-of\-work events and socials to get to know your team better * Good office locations with plenty of opportunity to socialise outside of work **Inclusion and belonging** We’re proud holders of Insurance Business Magazine’s 5\-Star Inclusion and Belonging Employer award. Through our dedicated Inclusion and Belonging committee, we hold educational events that foster a culture of curiosity, not judgement. * A dedicated Inclusion and Belonging Committee, supported by our Global Advisory Board * Inclusive policies and procedures to ensure all of our people are treated fairly * Access to Business Resource Groups that can support with multiple of key challenges **NFP and You... Better Together!** NFP is an inclusive Equal Employment Opportunity employer.

title: Investment Analyst - Investor Development Programme
description: **Necessary arrangements can be made at interview stage to accommodate the needs of candidates with disabilities. If you are having difficulty uploading your CV online or in fully accessing any information on this site please email** **Role Profile** **Investment Analyst \- Investor Development Programme** **Job title: Investment Analyst** **Division: Ireland Strategic Investment Fund (ISIF)** **Reports to: Investment Manager** **Contract: 3 Year FTC** * Closing Date for Applications: Wednesday, 29th April 2026\*\* **Summary Of The Business** The National Treasury Management Agency (NTMA) provides asset and liability management services to the Irish Government. It has evolved from a single function agency managing the National Debt to a manager of a complex portfolio of public assets and liabilities. Businesses managed by the NTMA include borrowing for the Exchequer and the management of the National Debt, the State Claims Agency, the New Economy and Recovery Authority (“NewERA”), the Ireland Strategic Investment Fund, National Development Finance Agency and the NTMA Future Ireland Funds business unit with responsibility for the management of the Future Ireland Fund and Infrastructure, Climate and Nature Fund. The NTMA also assigns staff to the National Asset Management Agency, the Strategic Banking Corporation of Ireland (SBCI) and Home Building Finance Ireland (HBFI). Working at the NTMA offers excellent benefits, including: * Career average Defined Benefit Pension Scheme * Flexible work options, including hybrid working (3 day per week on site required) and variable work hours * An extensive wellbeing programme * Excellent learning and development opportunities that allow for full career development within the organisation * A diverse range of initiatives: LGBT\+, Gender Matters, Disability Awareness, Sports \& Social, Volunteering, and lots more * Reimbursement of annual professional membership fees ISIF is Ireland’s sovereign development fund which invests on a commercial basis in a manner designed to support economic activity and employment in Ireland. ISIF’s strategy focuses on four key investment themes: Climate \& Energy, Housing and Enabling Investments, Indigenous Businesses, and Food and Agriculture. ISIF invests across a variety of sectors, instruments (e.g. equity or debt) and asset classes (e.g. venture capital, private equity). See www.isif.ie for more detail. **Role Summary** ISIF is seeking to hire up to four Investment Analysts, which provides a unique opportunity for talented and motivated individuals to join a team tasked with the delivery of ISIF’s investment strategy and to gain exposure to the highest standards of the investment management industry. It is an ideal opportunity for high\-calibre candidates with some professional experience to either transition into an investment\-focused role or to further develop their investment skills and experience in a sovereign fund with a broad focus. The role will offer exposure to a broad range of industries and asset classes, including venture capital, growth equity, private equity and private credit. The role will have a high level of investment exposure from the outset, and successful candidates can expect to be an integral member of a team focused on one of ISIF’s four priority areas (i.e. Climate \& Energy, Housing and Enabling Investments, Indigenous Businesses, and Food and Agriculture). If you have a strong preference for any particular area, please note that when applying for the role. In addition to on\-the\-job training and experience, successful candidates will benefit from ISIF’s Investor Development Programme which is designed to provide training and development opportunities to support their professional and personal development. This is expected to include participation in internal and external training courses on relevant finance and investment matters, personal development opportunities and an ability to participate in or leverage the training opportunities made available by global asset managers or banks that ISIF has relationships with. ISIF’s Investor Development Programme will be three years in duration and successful candidates will join ISIF on a three\-year fixed\-term contract. All members of the ISIF team are required to remain flexible to respond to the dynamic nature of the ISIF mandate – this may require team members to apply their skills to other types of investment, outside of their core areas of responsibility, from time to time. **Principal Accountabilities** The candidate will be required to work alongside other Investment Analysts, Managers and Directors across the full range of the ISIF’s investment activities. The principal responsibilities will include: * Due diligence and the preparation of investment committee materials for new investments * Industry and company research and analysis * Financial analysis, including assisting with financial modelling and valuations as required, to support the evaluation of investment opportunities * Engaging with third\-party advisors (including on their appointment and scope) * Project management of deal execution * Working collaboratively with clients, co\-investors, advisers and internal stakeholders to create, execute and/or manage investments **Experience** The role is open to all talented and motivated individuals interested in transitioning into an investment role or further developing their skills in that area. The ideal candidate will likely have 2 to 4 years of professional experience. Working in corporate finance, investment appraisal or experience of commercial or financial due diligence will be a distinct advantage. **Knowledge** The ideal candidate will: * Have a third level qualification in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Scientific, Engineering or a related discipline * Demonstrate a keen and well\-informed interest in investment and/or in developing knowledge and expertise in investment **Skills** * Excellent overall academic profile with a desire to pursue professional qualifications * Diligent, self\-motivated and proactive * Displays a high level of accuracy, efficiency and attention to detail in all aspects of work * Excellent communication skills, with the ability to convey complex information clearly and concisely, both verbally and in writing * Strong IT proficiency, including in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) * Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to work within a small team * Ability to manage a range of tasks across several stakeholders in a professional manner **Pension Abatement** If an appointee has previously been employed in the Civil or Public Service and that appointee is entitled to or in receipt of a pension from the Civil or Public Service or where a Civil/Public Service pension comes into payment during the appointee’s re\-employment that pension will be subject to abatement in accordance with Section 52 of the Public Service Pensions (Single Scheme and other Provisions) Act 2012\. Please note: In applying for this position, you are acknowledging that you understand that the abatement provisions, where relevant, will apply. It is not envisaged that the employing Department/Office/Body will support an application for an abatement waiver in respect of appointments to this position. Applicants will be required to complete a pre\-employment declaration to confirm whether they have previously availed of a Public sector pension scheme. The NTMA is proud to be an equal opportunities employer. We recognise that a diverse and inclusive workplace informs better decision making, creative thinking, innovation and drives business performance. We are strongly committed to ensuring that our workforce is representative of our society. Necessary arrangements can be made at interview stage to accommodate the needs of candidates with disabilities. If you are having difficulty uploading your CV online or in fully accessing any information on this site please email recruitment@ntma.ie **The NTMA is proud to be an equal opportunities employer. We recognise that a diverse and inclusive workplace informs better decision making, creative thinking, innovation and drives business performance. We are strongly committed to ensuring that our workforce is representative of our society.**

title: Junior Ecologist
description: The Junior Ecologist supports the delivery of ecological surveys, assessments, and reporting across a wide range of infrastructure, energy, renewables, and development projects. The role provides an opportunity to develop core consultancy skills while working as part of multidisciplinary project teams under the guidance of senior and principal ecologists. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ ### **Field Survey Support** * Assist with ecological field surveys including habitat surveys, protected species surveys, and monitoring work (e.g. GCN, bats, birds, otter, water vole – depending on experience and season). * Support data collection, recording, and interpretation in line with recognised survey guidance. * Follow health, safety, and biosecurity procedures during fieldwork. ### **Assessment \& Reporting** * Support the preparation of technical reports, including: + Ecological Impact Assessments (EcIA) + EIA ecology chapters + Baseline reports and survey summaries * Assist with data analysis, mapping, and presentation of findings. * Contribute to mitigation measures and recommendations under supervision. ### **Project Support** * Work within project teams to deliver ecology inputs to agreed scope, programme, and budget. * Assist with project administration, scheduling surveys, and coordination with colleagues. * Support senior staff in responding to client queries and regulator requests. ### **Learning \& Development** * Develop technical knowledge of UK wildlife legislation, planning policy, and best‑practice guidance. * Participate in training, mentoring, and technical development activities. * Build competency towards independent survey and reporting capability. ### **Collaboration \& Culture** * Work collaboratively with ecologists, EIA practitioners, planners, and other specialists. * Contribute positively to team culture and knowledge sharing. * Uphold ERM values, quality standards, and commitment to sustainability. **Skills \& Experience** ------------------------ ### **Essential** * Degree in Ecology, Environmental Science, or a related discipline. * Basic understanding of UK ecology, habitats, and wildlife legislation. * Willingness to undertake fieldwork, including early starts and occasional travel. * Strong attention to detail and good written communication skills. * Ability to work effectively as part of a team. * Full UK driving licence (or working towards). ### **Desirable** * Some experience of ecological field surveys (e.g. through placements, volunteering, or previous roles). * Familiarity with ecological survey methods and reporting. * Interest in developing specialist skills (e.g. ornithology, freshwater ecology, protected species). * Membership (or working towards membership) of a relevant professional body (e.g. CIEEM). * Basic GIS or data handling skills. **Behaviours \& Attributes** ---------------------------- * Curious and keen to learn. * Reliable, organised, and proactive. * Positive attitude to feedback and development. * Commitment to high standards of safety and quality. * Passion for ecology and environmental protection. **Reporting \& Support** ------------------------ * Reports to: Senior Ecologist / Principal Ecologist / Ecology Team Lead * Works closely with: CPD project teams, EIA practitioners, and other technical disciplines **Why Join ERM as a Junior Ecologist** -------------------------------------- This role offers structured development within a leading global environmental consultancy, exposure to nationally significant projects, and a clear pathway for progression to Consultant and Senior Ecologist roles. \#LI\-SD1 \#LI\-Hydrid Candidates may be invited to complete an online assessment as part of our recruitment process. All personal information will be handled confidentially and in compliance with data protection laws. ⠀ ERM is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to thrive, it’s an essential part of what makes ERM a great place to build a career and helps us create better solutions for our clients. We welcome talent from all backgrounds and provide equal opportunities for every candidate. If you have a disability, are neurodivergent, or need accommodations during the selection process, we’re here to support you. Our commitment doesn’t stop at hiring. Once you join us, we’ll ensure you have the tools, support, and adjustments needed to succeed and feel a true sense of belonging. Learn more about our Diversity, Inclusion \& Belonging (DIB) efforts by visiting our website or exploring our 2025 Sustainability Report..

A strong ethos on health and safety, compliance, reporting, and documentation are essential qualities. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** * Provide health and safety training to employees including but not limited to manual handling, toolbox talks and safety induction. * Previous experience conducting safety inspections and audits. * Approaches accident prevention and protection of the environment in a positive manner with the ability to initiate and maintain this positive manner with effective and efficient measures to raise safety, health and environmental standards and awareness. * Assist in the preparation of reports on occurrences and provide statistical information to management. * Implement the company's health, safety and environmental policy, procedures, and management systems. * Assist in the maintenance of Health \& Safety KPIs to help drive improvement in all related matters. * Support management in the development of H\&S procedures, policies, and programs. * Work as part of the HSQE team to ensure the provision of a safe and productive work environment. * Always seek to improve the standard of Health \& Safety in a practical manner within the company management system and on specific sites. * Monitor and enforce the implementation of Health \& Safety Management System. * Identify site specific hazards and ensure they are addressed in the site documentation and in a practical manner on the site. * Ensure clear lines of communication are maintained to the H\&S Manager. * Ensure the site folders and statutory records are being maintained. * Ensure strict adherence to site rules by all. * Assist site management to comply with statutory H\&S requirements. * Assist Health \& Safety Manager to develop and implement Risk assessments and improvements to the Safe Systems of Work. * Assist H\&S Manager and Site management to coordinate the safe systems of work for all. * Carry out H\&S auditing on site and address non\-conformances as they are identified with site management and contractors. * Contribute to accident and incident investigation. * Identify training needs on site for GMC personnel and contractors. * Ad hoc tasks as needed by the wider business. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** * Full clean driver's license essential. * A 3rd level qualification in Health and Safety is desirable. * 1\+ years experience in a H\&S role. * Civils, drainage or utilities (water \& electricity) experience. * Confident, motivated and can work independently as well as part of a team. * Ability to work under pressure with strict deadlines. * Time management abilities to meet targets. * Good communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills. * Integrity and respect for confidentiality and privacy. * Strong relationship builder and ability to leverage key relationships across the full spectrum of project stakeholders and statutory bodies. * Ability to manage risk and implement control measures. * Able to work and build strong relationships with contractors from various backgrounds and trades. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

This is a great opportunity for someone with around 1\-2 years' IT support experience who is looking to build their career in a busy, hands\-on environment. The role is focused on day\-to\-day user support, responding to tickets, troubleshooting common IT issues, setting up devices, and helping staff both remotely and on site. You will work closely with the wider IT team and play an important role in keeping users productive and IT services running smoothly. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** * Provide first\-line IT support to staff across multiple office locations * Log, triage, and manage incidents and service requests through the IT ticketing system * Support users by email, phone, remote support tools, and in person * Troubleshoot day\-to\-day issues relating to laptops, desktops, printers, mobile phones, tablets, and other end\-user devices * Set up, deploy, and maintain user devices and equipment * Assist with user account administration and general Microsoft 365 / Windows support tasks * Maintain accurate device and asset records * Escalate more complex issues to senior members of the IT team where required * Assist with routine IT maintenance tasks, software updates, and general housekeeping * Support small IT projects and office visits as needed **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** * Approximately 1\-2 years' experience in an IT support or service desk role * Good working knowledge of Windows and Microsoft Office / Microsoft 365 * A solid understanding of core IT fundamentals is essential, including IP addressing, DNS and DHCP. Candidates should understand how these technologies enable devices and systems to communicate across a network. * Experience supporting end users with common hardware and software issues * Strong communication skills and a helpful, professional approach * Ability to manage and prioritise workload through a ticketing system * A genuine interest in IT and a willingness to learn and develop * Ability to work well as part of a team and use initiative when required * Full driving licence and own transport, as occasional travel to other office locations may be required. (Travel expenses will be paid per a kilometre travelled) **Desirable** * Exposure to Active Directory, Office 365 administration, basic networking, and remote support tools * Experience with device builds, patching, or asset management * IT certifications such as CompTIA A\+, Network\+, CCNA or Microsoft certifications **What The Role Offers** * A hands\-on opportunity to build broad IT support experience * Exposure to a wide range of technologies and day\-to\-day operational support * The chance to develop your skills within a busy internal IT team * Ongoing learning and practical experience across multiple office environments * A strong culture of continuous learning, with encouragement for team members to pursue relevant certifications and further develop their technical skills **As occasional travel to company locations is required, candidates must hold a full clean driving licence valid for use in Ireland, or be willing to obtain one within the first 6\-9 months of employment.** **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Junior Transaction Reporting Analyst
description: **Role Overview:** The Junior Transaction Reporting Analyst will support the firm’s daily transaction and trade reporting obligations, ensuring timely, accurate, and compliant submissions to regulators while adhering to the relevant controls. The role also offers exposure to regulatory change, process improvement, and broader reporting initiatives, providing a strong foundation for development within regulatory reporting. As you progress in the role, you will contribute to supporting the Co\-Heads of Regulatory Reporting in the ongoing enhancement of the firm’s transaction reporting framework, support the assessment and implementation of new regulatory requirements, and assist with wider regulatory and business projects across the firm. **About the Team:** The Transaction Reporting Team is responsible for the completion and monitoring of several transaction reporting obligations to the Central Bank of Ireland and to the relevant Trade Repositories. These include MiFID Transaction Reporting, MiFID Post Trade Transparency, EMIR Data Reporting and SFTR Transaction Reporting. The team is also involved in the firm Shareholding Disclosure Obligations (including substantial shareholding rules, takeover disclosure and short selling), and in other regulatory obligations (i.e. monthly metrics). **Main Responsibilities:** * Prepare and submit transaction and trade reports to the relevant National Competent Authorities within required timelines * Investigate trading activities and identify reportable transactions. * Perform and document reporting controls to ensure data accuracy and completeness. * Reconcile and compare data sets between Trade Repository and internal systems, identifying mismatches and proactively solving the breaks. * Develop relationships with internal stakeholders, including Front Office, Compliance, Operations and NBC Group teams to adapt reporting solutions with the evolving nature of the business. **Skills and Experience Required:** * Advanced Microsoft Excel and VBA skills. * Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration or Law * Minimum 1 years’ experience in the financial services industry; regulatory or reporting exposure is beneficial * Previous experience with transaction reporting (MiFIR, EMIR and/or SFTR), across asset classes (equity, credit, foreign exchange) centrally cleared and OTC is advantageous. * Ability to work independently, use initiative and perform well under pressure to meet deadlines * Experience in the independent assessment of new Regulations to ensure compliance with the firm’s business practices and experience of adapting the reporting framework to new trading activities. **About Us:** NBCGF is wholly owned direct subsidiary of National Bank of Canada (“NBC”). The main strategic purpose of NBCGF is to enable the NBC Group (“NBC Group,” meaning National Bank of Canada and all its subsidiaries) to grow and diversify its presence in Europe while allowing for a natural development of its existing business relationships and the establishment of new ones in the European markets. NBC has over 150 years of history and is currently the 6th largest Canadian bank. Through its branch and subsidiary network, NBC can service clients throughout North America and Europe. NBC’s strategy is to further develop NBCGF as its hub for trading and financial services in Europe. **What NBCGF has to Offer:** Access to competitive working conditions, a wide range of employee benefits, and a dynamic work environment. Open, respectful workplace where all employees can achieve their full potential. NBCGF recognises that the culture of an organisation and the performance of its workforce are not mutually exclusive. Since our inception in 2013, NBCGF has worked tirelessly to ensure our cultural framework aligns not only with the Code of Conduct and Ethics of our parent company, NBC, but with our own environmental and local values. Promoting and supporting diversity in the workplace is an important aspect of good people’s management\- it’s about valuing everyone in the organisation as an individual. We at NBCGF have an effective diversity and inclusion approach concerning our employees. There are no silos in our teams and management strive to add value to our organisation, by contributing to employee well\-being and engagement. NBCGF takes pride in its diverse work force, employing and cultivating professionals from all over the globe. Working at NBCGF can take different forms and it is built around the relationship of trust between the employer and its employees. NBCGF operates a hybrid working model where the employee’s time can be split between the office and a remote location on a schedule which may vary. The model considers customer expectations, organisational needs, and constraints, as well as the employees’ need for flexibility and the nature of the work they perform. What is important is having the right arrangements in place so that you can best perform your role. We're putting people first. We're a bank on a human scale that stands out for its courage, entrepreneurial culture, and passion for people. Our mission is to have a positive impact on people’s lives. We welcome all candidates! What can you bring to our team?

title: Keyholder (Full time) - Kildare Village
description: ### **ABOUT THE POSITION** With over 430 retail stores worldwide, our associates are essential to our business. From ensuring product availability on the sales floor to creating eye\-catching visual displays to addressing customer inquiries to running an efficient check out process, our associates create memorable customer experiences while simultaneously assisting the Store Leadership team support Columbia Sportswear Company’s mission of “Connecting Active People with Their Passions.” As a Retail Associate II you will serve as a brand ambassador to provide exceptional customer service; contributing to the profitability and customer experience in the store. Within this role you will employ your natural problem solving and sales abilities to answer customer questions and make compelling product recommendations while supporting Columbia Sportswear Companies values to enhance customer service and teamwork. The Retail Associate II role is only used for Retail Associates who are in the Designated Key Holder Program. This role is based at our outlet store in **Kildare, Ireland** . Applicants must have the legal right to work in Ireland ( **a valid work permit is required** ). ### **HOW YOU’LL MAKE A DIFFERENCE** * Provide exceptional customer service by responding to consumer questions or issues regarding product features, benefits and availability; engage with consumers to understand their needs and preferences (e.g., demonstrate and explain merchandise, make suggestions on suitable options for consumers etc.). * Maintain store standards for visual merchandising, cleanliness, safety; retrieves merchandise from sales floor, stock room, or other inventory locations. * Perform all required cash register transactions (e.g., sales, returns, exchanges, connecting consumer to loyalty program) efficiently and accurately. * Contribute to store profitability by receiving, handling, replenishing and processing incoming and outgoing product flow in the stores accurately and efficiently * Perform designated keyholder duties as needed which may include opening and closing store duties. ### **YOU HAVE** * No specific education required (High School Diploma or GED preferred) * 2 (or less) years of experience in position or specialization * Skill operating a Point of Sales (POS) system, other in\-store computerized systems, and telephone ### **JOB CONDITIONS** * Frequently in a more active environment (i.e., requires ability to constantly move about) that can be physical or strenuous in nature (frequently adjusting/moving items weighing up to 40 lbs.), may frequently use specialized equipment, may have exposure to dust and noise * Occasionally requires the ability to work in place * Ability to clearly communicate with others * Availability to work a flexible retail schedule, which may change seasonally and in response to location\-specific business trends or forecasts. All local, state, and federal laws are adhered to when evaluating your availability. *This job description is not meant to be an all\-inclusive list of duties and responsibilities, but constitutes a general definition of the position's scope and function in the company.*

title: Knowledge Lawyer (Finance)
description: We are a leading international law and professional services firm providing legal, corporate and fiduciary services to global corporations, financial institutions, capital market participants and investment fund managers. With a global presence spanning the Americas, Europe, the Middle East and Asia, we advise on the laws of Bermuda, the British Virgin Islands, the Cayman Islands, Guernsey, Ireland and Jersey. We treat everyone as the intelligent professional they are. Our approach is to trust and empower our people to deliver consistently, and enable them to succeed. Diversity is our secret weapon – it’s the sheer breadth of Walkers people that makes us who we are – gathered from across the globe and fluent in languages, jurisdictions and cultures that help us to mirror our clients and keep our own thinking in tune with the world in which we operate. **The Role** Walkers is currently seeking to recruit an experienced and enthusiastic Knowledge Lawyer to join our highly regarded Finance and Capital Markets team in Dublin, on a permanent and full\-time basis (5 days). The option of a 4 day working week may be considered for the right candidate. Hybrid working options and flexible hours available. This is an exciting new role in our market\-leading and award\-winning structured finance \& securitisation practice. The successful candidate will work closely with the partners in the Irish office to help ensure that the team's knowledge and development objectives are achieved. We advise clients on the full range of banking, debt capital markets, finance, private credit, fund finance, real estate finance, securitisation and structured credit transactions. Your work will be interesting, rewarding, challenging and impactful. This is an excellent opportunity to work in an internationally focused, professional organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous personal professional development, mentoring and career development within a collegiate team\-based environment. The successful candidate will join our existing KM team and will support the strategies and processes to develop and maintain legal and professional resources including: * precedents; * know\-how; * client\-facing guides; * practice notes; * management of knowledge dissemination to Walkers lawyers; and * familiarity with Walkers external resources (for example library, journal subscriptions and online resources), In addition, they will also have the opportunity to support the Legal and Technical Training Manager in the development and delivery of training to the fee earners to enable them to effectively meet the needs of the clients in an evolving legal and regulatory landscape. This role offers the opportunity to drive excellence within the practice group while working closely with partners and senior knowledge lawyers to deliver high\-impact knowledge management and training for a high\-performing, dynamic and fast\-paced team. **Reporting** The role will report to the Co\-Heads of the FCM group. **Key Focus Areas** * Banking \& Finance * Debt Capital Markets * Derivatives \& risk transfer arrangements * Fund Finance * Green Finance and ESG products * Insurance\-Linked Securities * Private Credit * Real Estate Finance * Securitisation including ABS, CMBS, CLOs, RMBS \& synthetic securitisations * Securities law and regulatory considerations * Structured Credit * Structured Products * Company law **Duties \& Responsibilities** **Precedents and Know\-How** * Impact Assessments: monitoring and carrying out impact assessments on the implications of new legal and regulatory developments, identifying potential risks and opportunities for clients * Precedents \& Protocols: assisting with the maintenance and development of the Firm’s precedents, processes and knowledge systems, including automated documentation on DocumentDrafter, and providing related training and support where required * Current Awareness: management of timely knowledge dissemination to relevant fee earners * Horizon Scanning: reviewing existing, new and proposed legislation and case law, keeping abreast of developments in Irish \& EU legislation generally and disseminating the information thereon to relevant fee earners and other relevant practice groups **Training** * New Joiner Training: designing and maintaining suitable pre\-joining materials to be provided to new staff and assisting with the training of new fee earners in relation to the legal resources available, precedents, documentation standards \& protocols and other relevant knowledge materials * Legal Resources Training: train new lawyers, secretaries and corporate services executives on the availability of legal resources * Fee Earner Training: assisting with the coordination of a team\-wide training programme and assisting where possible with targeted training including the delivery of training sessions to fee earners on relevant aspects of Irish \& EU law and in relation to legal developments * Client Training: Preparing and delivering high\-quality briefing papers and presentations for clients on relevant legal and regulatory topics **Management and Administration** * Actively participating in the evaluation and implementation of legal technology solutions to improve knowledge management processes, internal protocols and to enhance research efficiency within the Firm * Support the development and/or reinforcement of relationships with stakeholder practice groups and offices through a variety of channels including face\-to\-face meetings, regular group calls, attending wider practice group meetings, social events and incentive\-driven projects. * Support the development and/or reinforcement of relationships with other business services departments (for example, preparing first drafts of thought leadership pieces/directory entries with the marketing and business development teams). * Support as directed the activities of the various Precedents and Know\-How Committees and/or the Global Opinion Committee, as applicable, as well as other working groups and committees by specific request. **Business Development** * Assisting in the production and/or updating of relevant Guides, thought leadership pieces, articles and practice notes * Liaising with senior fee earners and the Business Development Group in relation to website updates, thought leadership pieces, legal journal submissions etc **Skills \& Personal Attributes** The successful candidate should be a qualified solicitor or barrister in Ireland, Northern Ireland or England \& Wales with at least 4\-5 years PQE, ideally with a leading Irish or international law firm or in independent practice in the Law Library, or equivalent, with experience in one or more of finance, capital markets, structured finance \& securitisation, regulatory \& compliance, or general corporate. Candidate should have a proven strong academic track record including at least a 2:1 honours degree in any discipline. We are looking for candidates who can demonstrate the following: **Essential** * collegiate and thoughtful team player; * strong academic and experience credentials; * excellent research, analytical, technical, drafting skills and attention to detail; * an in\-depth knowledge of the types of resources for which a Knowledge Lawyer is responsible (for example, library resources, journals and online resources) and strong understanding of the importance of effective knowledge management within a law firm, with a particular focus on the needs of a busy Finance department; * client focused and commercial aptitude with an interest in/experience of business development and growing a service offering; * a calm and measured approach to problem solving; * excellent oral and written communication skills and ability to tailor this to communicate with partners, colleagues and (where appropriate) with the Firm's clients; * ability to work under pressure and responsive to the expectations of partners and clients; * able to prioritise competing demands; * excellent time management, project management and organisational skills; and * participate in continuously improving services through critically evaluating systems of work, promoting new ideas and actively developing service improvements. **Desirable:** * experience in Banking Asset Finance or Finance \& Capital Markets desirable, but not essential * experience with traditional online resources including BetterRegulation, WestLaw IE, Practical Law (PLC), Bloomsbury, Lexis\+, LMA online etc; * experience with Harvey AI, Co\-Pilot and other legal AI tech; * strong experience with Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and MS Excel. * knowledge of SharePoint, document management and document automation software an advantage. **Benefits** We offer excellent progression opportunities along with a competitive remuneration package and benefits including: * Competitive Salary * Supportive, collegiate environment and open culture * Prominent international clients and challenging work * Working directly with partners * 'Dress for your day' dress code * Discretionary annual bonus scheme; * Generous annual leave * Agile Working Policy * Health insurance cover * Pension scheme and firm contribution * Life Assurance * Paid Maternity \& Paternity leave * Income Protection * Taxsaver and Bike to Work schemes * Professional Membership Fees paid * Learning \& Development and Mentorship programmes * Educational bursaries and assistance * Client secondment opportunities * Social Committee, DEI Committee, CSR Committee and various sports, social and charity initiatives * Subsidised gym access/fitness classes * Employee Assistance Programme * VDU eye testing and medical screening * Wellness programme Walkers global is an equal opportunity employer. Equality and diversity are key to our global identity and an integral part of our goal to continue being an employer of choice. We are committed to a work environment that supports all individuals irrespective of gender, ethnicity, nationality, race, religion, marital status, age, disability, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other applicable legally protected characteristics. We make every effort to ensure that employment opportunities are open and accessible to all purely on the basis of personal ability.

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: We are currently working with a leading organisation in a regulated manufacturing environment to hire a Plant Engineer for their Dublin site. This is a key hire within the engineering team, where you will play a lead role supporting the Maintenance Manager while taking ownership of site\-wide equipment across process, utilities, and tank farm areas. The position offers strong technical exposure along with the opportunity to drive reliability, improvements, and engineering standards across the site. **The Role:** * Act as the technical expert for your area, ensuring optimal performance of process and utility equipment * Lead maintenance activities across pumps, motors, HVAC, PLC\-controlled systems and instrumentation * Diagnose and resolve mechanical, electrical and automation faults, minimising downtime * Manage and coordinate service providers and contractors on\-site * Support and plan electrical shutdowns in line with safety and operational requirements * Lead root cause analysis on equipment failures and implement corrective actions * Drive continuous improvement initiatives across maintenance and reliability * Ensure full compliance with EHS and GMP standards, including accurate documentation **What We Are Looking For:** * 3\+ years’ experience in a plant/equipment engineering role within a regulated environment * Strong exposure to utilities and process systems such as HVAC, purified water, clean steam or refrigeration * Hands\-on experience troubleshooting across mechanical, electrical and automation systems * Experience working in a GMP environment with strong compliance awareness * Engineering qualification (degree or equivalent experience)

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Maintenance Supervisor
description: **Maintenance Supervisor** Carlton Hotel Blanchardstown \& Carlton Hotel Dublin Airport – Dublin **Why Join Us** * Competitive salary (DOE) * Performance Bonus Scheme (linked to individual and team performance) * Free on\-site parking * Structured, supportive, and performance\-driven environment * Staff meals on duty \& uniform provided * Employee engagement initiatives and social events **About the Role** Carlton Hotels are seeking a Maintenance Supervisor to support the Facilities Manager in overseeing maintenance operations across our hotel group. This position contributes directly to: * Guest satisfaction and comfort * Health \& safety compliance * Operational efficiency and cost control * Preventative maintenance and asset longevity You will work closely with all departments, providing technical support while coordinating maintenance activities in a fast\-paced hotel environment. **Performance Bonus Scheme** * Bonus linked to individual performance and hotel results * Clear, measurable KPIs — you know exactly how to earn it * Key focus areas: * Response times \& job completion efficiency * Preventative maintenance delivery * Guest satisfaction (maintenance\-related feedback) * Health \& safety compliance * Cost control and resource management * Direct impact on earnings based on performance and results **Key Responsibilities** * Carry out and supervise day\-to\-day maintenance across hotel facilities (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, general repairs) * Conduct regular inspections to identify maintenance needs and potential risks equipment * Assist with event setup and breakdown as required * Support emergency procedures and respond effectively to incidents * Work collaboratively with Front Office, Accommodation, and other departments **The Ideal Candidate** Essential * Minimum 2 years’ experience in maintenance, ideally within a hotel or hospitality environment * Strong technical knowledge across general maintenance (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, etc.) * Proven ability to troubleshoot and resolve maintenance issues efficiently * Good understanding of health \& safety and compliance requirements * Strong organisational and problem\-solving skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Good communication and interpersonal skills * Basic computer skills for record\-keeping Desirable * Previous supervisory or team\-leading experience * Experience in a multi\-site or hotel group environment * Knowledge of maintenance management systems **Personal Attributes** * Hands\-on, proactive, and solution\-focused * High attention to detail * Calm and efficient under pressure * Strong work ethic and reliability * Flexible approach to working hours (including weekends if required) * Guest\-focused mindset with minimal disruption approach to works **Location** Carlton Hotel Blanchardstown \& Carlton Hotel Dublin Airport **Job Type** Full\-time, Permanent **Start Date** Immediate Carlton Hotels is an Equal Opportunities Employer. **INDHP** Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €32,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Cloghran, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Experience: * Maintenance: 2 years (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Marketing & Communications Intern
description: **Who We Are** Irish Hospice Foundation (IHF) is a national charity that addresses dying, death and bereavement in Ireland. Our vision is an Ireland where people facing end of life or bereavement, and those who care for them, are provided with the care and support that they need. Through advocacy and education, and the provision of services such as Nurses for Night Care and the Bereavement Support Line and our national programmes such as Hospice Friendly Hospitals, Dying Well at Home and Caru – Supporting Care Compassion at End of Life in Nursing Homes, Adult Bereavement Programme and Irish Childhood Bereavement Network, IHF works to ensure that every person in Ireland can die and grieve well, whatever their age and wherever the place. **About The Role** **Role Purpose:** Our Marketing Communications team forms a central role in promoting conversations on dying, death and bereavement and our Marketing Communications Intern will support the team in achieving the clear and effective communication and marketing initiatives in both digital and traditional channels. The successful candidate will support the team in developing relevant content and in the planning of relevant events for a wide variety of audiences. The role presents an exciting opportunity for a highly motivated person to gain valuable early\-career experience working as part of a busy team. **Salary:** €15 an hour (37 hours per week) **Contract Type:** 13\-week Fixed Term Contact (2nd June – 29th August) **Location:** Our offices are based on Nassau St., Dublin 2\. We have a hybrid work model in place (a minimum requirement of 2 days a week in the office). **Reporting to:** Director of Marketing Communications **Responsibilities** * Supporting in the delivery of marketing and communications activities for Irish Hospice Foundation in a range of ways, including but not limited to: * Content creation: developing engaging content for IHF for use on digital and traditional media channels. * Social media calendar support: assisting in the delivery of effective messaging mix, channel mix and timing delivery. * Digital marketing: Working with the digital lead on effective website engagement strategies. * Event co\-ordination: Assisting with the planning and execution of key events and supporting the team in preparing effective marketing materials to promote and support event delivery. * Administrative support: providing support for marketing and communication initiatives, including scheduling meetings, taking minutes and supporting the development of key reports. Any other tasks as may be assigned by the manager or designated person. **Qualifications And Experience** This role will offer a suitable candidate excellent hands\-on experience in marketing and communications but requires the candidate to have a level of knowledge that will enable them to effectively engage with the role, for this reason we are seeking a candidate who has gained knowledge and experience of marketing and communications and has either recently completed a degree programme or will be commencing the final year of their studies in September 2026\. **Essential** * Excellent written and language skills * Excellent communication skills * Proficient in use of IT systems Office 365 and relevant social media platforms. * Awareness of the work of Irish Hospice Foundation * Recent relevant references **Desirable** * Proficiency in Canva * Experience in a website platform such as Wordpress **Application Procedure** Closing date for applications is **Wednesday** **29th April 2026** Applicants should submit a CV (max. 2 pages), detailing relevant experience, along with a covering letter outlining the reasons they believe they are suitable for this role. * Applicants will be short\-listed based on information supplied. * Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted. * Should the person recommended for appointment decline, or having accepted it, relinquish it, or if an additional vacancy arises, IHF may, at its discretion, select and recommend another person for appointment on the results of this selection process. Candidates must be legally entitled to work in Ireland at the time of application. If you require accommodations to be made during the recruitment process, or have questions relating to accessibility, please contact us. Interviews for this role are likely to take place the first week in May 2026\.

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: **Key Responsibilities:** * Ensure technical compliance across all assigned projects * Manage and deliver mechanical design scope * Coordinate consultant selection and define roles/responsibilities (RACI) * Participate in design team meetings * Align design programmes with project planning schedules * Review and coordinate client responsibility matrices with project teams * Manage design processes through RIBA Stages 3–5 * Develop and implement project design management plans * Ensure engineering standards and checklists are applied * Support design for manufacture and assembly where required * Maintain and manage key project documentation and trackers, including procurement schedules, technical submittals, drawings, RFIs, design changes, deviations, and lessons learned * Monitor project progress and carry out regular technical reviews * Support project teams with technical submissions, drawing reviews, and RFIs * Review procurement to ensure cost\-effective solutions * Contribute to value engineering initiatives * Ensure compliance with relevant industry standards (e.g. ISO19650\) * Produce regular project reports * Assist with tendering, estimating, and pre\-construction activities * Support continuous improvement and lessons learned processes * Mentor junior/graduate engineers * Attend site visits as required * Carry out additional design\-related duties as needed **Essential Criteria:** * Degree in Mechanical Engineering or similar * 3\+ years’ experience on large\-scale industrial projects (data centre/pharmaceutical) * Proficiency in mechanical design software * Strong communication skills * High attention to detail and quality of work * Ability to work from a central office location

title: Neuroadaptive Fitness Coach
description: **Empowering young legends of all abilities.** Lifting Legends is Wicklow’s newest and most exciting physical fitness centre, dedicated exclusively to children and teens with differing needs. We provide fun, inclusive, and adaptive health and fitness programs that build strength, confidence, and joy through movement, one lift at a time. Founded by Eoin Murphy, who has successfully run this specialised service in Blarney, Co. Cork for over 10 years, Lifting Legends is bringing the same passion, expertise, and fun to Greystones. **About the Role** We’re seeking passionate and energetic coaches who want to help deliver an ***industry\-leading experience*** for both our members and our team. Please note all potential hires require to have an Autism Level 1 (Pro) cert before starting with us. More information on this certification can be found below. https://www.autismfitness.com/Level1 If you are currently completing the course please still apply. In Lifting Legends you will work with children and teens with varying needs such as autism, Down syndrome, cerebral palsy, ADHD, PDA, anxiety, sensory challenges and muscle dyspraxia in ***small\-group (4\-on\-1*****)\* and \_**individual (1\-on\-1\)\*\_ sessions. Each session is designed to be safe, engaging, and empowering, helping every child move, grow, and thrive. You’ll receive full training and mentoring to help you deliver industry\-leading, inclusive coaching that changes lives. **Session Times:** Afternoons, evenings, and Saturday mornings/afternoons **Hours:** 8 – 15 hours per week **Pay:**€17 – €20 per hour (depending on experience) **What We’re Looking For** * Experience working with children (1 year required) * Experience working with individuals with differing needs (1 year required) * Coaching or personal training experience in a gym environment (essential) * Current personal indemnity and public liability insurance (required) * Garda Vetting (required) * Fluent English (required) **Be Part of Something Industry\-Leading** At Lifting Legends, we’re raising the bar for excellence, not only in how we support our members but also in how we support our team. We’re building a workplace where coaches are empowered, trained, and inspired to become the best in the industry. **Employee Benefits** We believe great coaches deserve an environment that looks after their well\-being, growth, and happiness. When you join Lifting Legends, you’ll enjoy: * Company events * Employee discount * Gym membership * On\-site gym * On\-site parking * Store discount **Application \& Interview Process** To be considered for this role, **all applicants must**: * Provide a current resume, and * Submit a response addressing the four Selection Criteria below, *or* * Alternatively, submit a video application (maximum 4 minutes) addressing the same criteria. During the interview process, we’ll discuss your responses to the selection criteria in more detail and explore your experience, coaching style, and approach to supporting children with additional needs. **Selection Criteria** * **Experience in Fitness and Coaching.** Tell us about your experience in the fitness industry and what makes you a great coach. Please include specific and recent examples that highlight your coaching style, results, and the impact you’ve had on your clients. * **Working with Children with Differing Needs:** Tell us about your experience working with children with differing needs. Please include specific and recent examples. If you haven’t had direct experience, explain how you would adapt your coaching approach to ensure every child feels safe, supported, and successful. * **Delivering an Industry\-Leading Customer Experience.** At Lifting Legends, our goal is to deliver an *industry\-leading customer experience*. How would you help us achieve this? Share examples of times you’ve gone above and beyond to create a positive, memorable experience for clients or families. * **Creating an Industry\-Leading Employee Experience.** At Lifting Legends, we’re just as passionate about creating an *industry\-leading employee experience*. What specific training, development, or mentoring would help you grow into one of the best coaches in the industry at delivering inclusive, engaging, and high\-quality fitness sessions? Please submit to info@liftinglegends.ie and video submissions to 089 254 1456\. Join us as we bring movement, confidence, and smiles to a new generation of legends. **Lifting Legends – Where every child is a legend.** Job Types: Part\-time, Volunteer, Graduate Pay: €17\.00\-€20\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company events * Employee discount * Gym membership * On\-site gym * On\-site parking * Store discount * Wellness program Ability to commute/relocate: * Greystones, County Wicklow: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Completed selection criteria questions in the job description?
